creator: fred@thegalacticzoo.com
text:
So anyone test on raspberry Pi 3 hardware BCM2835 SOC?  raspi4B?  Raspi400 Keyboard model sbc BCM2711.   https://FreeBSD.org/where  RPI3/4 binary image for BCM2735 or BCM2711 SOC chips.  
https://download.freebsd.org/releases/arm64/aarch64/ISO-IMAGES/14.1/
https://download.freebsd.org/releases/arm64/aarch64/ISO-IMAGES/14.1/FreeBSD-14.1-RELEASE-arm64-aarch64-RPI.img.xz 

Have Others tested this Marcos Devesas Campos 3 HDMI Audio patches to FreeBSD kernel source code /usr/src ?  D36431 D37878 D37879 patch file?
Have you built the kernel?
time make -j4 buildkernel KERNCONF=GENERIC-VCHIQ -DNO_CLEAN

Have you installed the newly built kernel?
make installkernel KERNCONF=GENERIC-VCHIQ  

If a custom kernel was built, KERNCONF must also be set to use the new custom kernel:

    cd /usr/src
    make installkernel KERNCONF=GENERIC-VCHIQ
    shutdown -r now
    cd /usr/src
    make installworld
    shutdown -r now

pkg install mpg123 # use this audio program as a simple test. Others can be used. Open a Web Browser like Falkon and play audio or play a video
mpg123 test_file.mp3
ITS ALIVE and WELL and SOUNDS GREAT!! Marcos Devesas Campos for the WIN!



~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
I Wonder, if HDMI Audio does or does NOT work on the new raspi5 BCM2712 running FreeBSD 14.1-RELEASE kernel image?  Has anybody tested a FreeBSD 14.1-CURRENT snapshot for HDMI Audio to be operational on a BCM2712 Raspi5 board?  Yes HDMI interface is different for a Raspi5 BCM2712 than a Raspi4B BCM2711.  So the same patch files that do work for BCM2711 Raspi4B, 400 keyboard sbc, probably do NOT WORK for a BCM2712 Raspi5 hardware SBC.

See below:
In learning and testing with these patch files, I am further along in knowledge.
https://reviews.freebsd.org/F75131370#30 Has a complete review to install patch files and for building and installing kernel.  See that post F7513137 for details.

ps.  Would be nice to have the extra serial ports on a BCM2711, operational with FreeBSD.  Specifically Uart3.  Works on Raspberry Pi O/S.  Does NOT WORK on FreeBSD 14.0-CURRENT.  Adding a UART3 DTB to the config.txt file does not make UART3 operational.  I do not see code supporting, UART2, UART3, UART4, UART5 in the BCM2711 Raspi4B hardware.
